title: perf(downloads): kill 35s post-download SHA256 re-read — hash while streaming
description:
User reported the model-install 'Verifying…' stage feels very slow, suspected unzipping all custom nodes serially. Investigated with temp timing logs (all tagged MPI-TEMP-TIMING in routes/downloadManager.js). MEASURED on a real local install of ILL Anime Beauty (6.6GB weight):
  sha256 ill-anime-beauty: 34814ms for 6617MB   ← 35s, THE villain
  node-loop total (2 nodes): 5ms                 ← nodes already on disk, nothing
  (no extract/pip lines — both nodes pre-installed)
Unzip theory is DEAD (MPI-248 already made extract native-tar ~476ms). The 'Verifying…' wall = _verifySha256 (downloadManager.js ~L578) fs.createReadStream re-reading the WHOLE downloaded file to hash it AFTER download, ~189 MB/s disk-read-bound. Fires once per fresh weight download at 100% with a dead indeterminate bar.

FIX: hash the bytes AS THEY STREAM instead of re-reading after. DownloaderHelper v2.1.11 exposes a first-class .pipe(stream) API (__startDownload pipes the response through registered pipes before the file write). Register a stream.Writable hash-sink on the 'download' event → hash.update per chunk → stash digest on 'end'. _verifySha256 compares the in-memory digest, skipping the file re-read. Result: 35s → ~0ms verify.

WHY SAFE:
  - NO RESUME EVER (MPI-258 Bug 2): every download is clean single-stream, partial scrubbed on start (download() → fs.remove(localPath); DO NOT add resumeIfFileExists). So the pipe sees every byte exactly once, in order → incremental hash is correct by construction.
  - RETRY: DHL clears __pipes=[] on error/retry and re-emits 'download' at each stream start → re-register the sink + reset the hash on the 'download' event. Covered.
  - FALLBACK KEPT: if no in-memory digest is present (unexpected/edge), _verifySha256 falls back to today's disk re-read. Zero behaviour loss.
  - REMOTE/POD: never runs this local download path (installCustomNodes=false, image-resident). Untouched.

FILES: routes/downloadManager.js (DownloadManager class: _bindEvents/_ensureDownloader wire the pipe + hash; _verifySha256 gains the in-memory fast path).

VERIFY: re-install one weight model locally, grep logs for the MPI-TEMP-TIMING sha256 line → should read ~0ms (or add a temp 'streamed-hash' timing). Then REVERT all MPI-TEMP-TIMING logs.

BITE-BACK WATCHLIST: if a future change re-introduces resume (Range/206) the streamed hash goes wrong — MUST then fall back to disk re-read on any resumed download. Keep the no-resume invariant OR gate the fast path on !wasResumed.
result: SHIPPED + user-verified locally. Streaming SHA256 (DHL .pipe Transform hash-sink) drops the post-download verify from 34814ms → 1ms on a 6.6GB weight (streamed=yes, hash matched). 4 real edits in routes/downloadManager.js (require nodeStream; constructor hash state; _bindEvents 'download'→Transform pipe + 'end'→digest; _verifySha256 in-memory fast path w/ disk fallback). Temp MPI-TEMP-TIMING logs reverted. NOT committed yet (shared tree).
